comment: kagura-agent v0.2 defense-in-depth seccomp profile. NOT a deny-by-default sandbox: it is defaultAction=ALLOW with an explicit deny-list, layered on --cap-drop ALL + --security-opt no-new-privileges + --read-only. Its job is to ensure the high-risk, mostly capability-INDEPENDENT syscalls below are denied even on a permissively-configured daemon (e.g. one defaulting to seccomp=unconfined). A full deny-by-default allow-list (Docker-default-equivalent) is a stronger but heavier future hardening. architectures is intentionally omitted so the runtime resolves these syscall names for whatever arch the container runs on.

syscalls.comment: EPERM on a hijacked agent's escape/info-leak primitives (perf_event_open/bpf/userfaultfd are cap-independent; the rest are belt-and-suspenders since --cap-drop ALL already gates them).
